programme.title: Pequeños gigantes
programme.desc: La competencia arranca y los seis talentosos escuadrones comienzan la lucha por alcanzar sus sueños. Los capitales se enfrentan en una divertida prueba de imitación.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.desc: La desgracia de un hombre que con fuerza y voluntad cambió su vida tratando de ayudar a sus seres amados.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.sub-title: Edición especial supremacistas: Las redes del odio
programme.desc: Se muestran a los nuevos supremacistas y se analiza la manera en la que utilizan los medios sociales para atraer a nuevos miembros.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.desc: La violencia se ha apoderado de las vidas de miles de mujeres que han sido asesinadas brutalmente por sus propias parejas, por lo que los movimientos contra feminicidios han incrementado considerablemente.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.desc: Algunos fueron perseguidos y separados de sus familias, otros llevados a campos de concentración y exterminados, hoy, algunos de los sobrevivientes narran el horror del holocausto judío.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.sub-title: La vida en juego: fútbol y violencia en la frontera
programme.desc: Un programa especial que muestra la vida de cuatro jugadores de fútbol americano en Brownsville, Texas, quienes se enfrentan a una dura realidad de violencia, pobreza, drogas, pero siempre con ilusión de una vida mejor.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.desc: Una mujer de buen corazón es asesinada por su esposo, un hombre manipulador y frío quien dejó abandonada a su propia hija en los brazos de su madre fallecida.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.sub-title: Narcosubmarinos: Viaje al fondo del mal
programme.desc: Por décadas el narcotráfico ha navegado sigilosamente por los mares más peligrosos para transportar su mercancía, poniendo en peligro la vida de sus tripulantes a cambio de dinero.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora
programme.desc: Una familia destruida tras un ataque inesperado habla sobre todo lo que sufrieron al perder a sus seres queridos en manos de organizaciones delictivas.

programme.title: Aquí y ahora: Los mirreyes
programme.desc: Los mirreyes son la nueva generación de los hijos de papi. Privilegiados y desafiantes, tratan con desprecio a quienes les sirven. Los secretos de los hijos de los ricos y los poderosos son la evidencia de la guerra de clases en el México de hoy.

programme.title: Wycliffe Gordon
programme.sub-title: Wynton Marsalis: In This House, on This Morning
programme.desc: The final concert of the Münchner Klaviersommer 1992 was by Wynton Marsalis and his septet. The 31-years-young star trumpeter performed one of his own jazz suites to the sold-out Philharmonie, under the title of ‘In This House, On This Morning'. The concert covers every jazz style, in composition and improvisation, and in both small and grand forms. Marsalis's Septet was made up of Wess Anderson (alto saxophone), Wycliffe Gordon (trombone), Reginald Veal (bass), Herlin Riley (drums), Todd Williams (tenor saxophone), and Eric Reed (piano).

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie, Tony Coe, Derek Humble & Billy Mitchell
programme.desc: American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Dizzy Gillespie (1917-1993) was one of the seminal figures of the bebop movement. He fuses all musical forms rooted in African culture, such as music from Cuba, Latin America and the Caribbean, into his music. On November 4, 1970 he played a concert in Denmark with the Kenny Clarke/Francy Boland Big Band, performing Con Alma, Brother K, Now Hear My Meanin', Manteca, Let Me Outta Here, and Things Are Here.

programme.title: Chet Baker
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Keith Jarrett
programme.desc: Lugano Estival Jazz is an annual open-air music festival that takes place over five days at the picturesque city of Lugano, Switzerland. Since 1979, this musical Mecca has been a must-attend event for all jazz and world music fans worldwide. On July 3, 1986, American pianist and composer Keith Jarrett is joined by bassist Gary Peacock and drummer Jack DeJohnette at Estival Jazz Lugano. Known as the Standards Trio, they put on an enthralling performance that shows these master musicians at their best.

programme.title: Christian McBride
programme.sub-title: Josh Evans - Ballad Of Ernie Washington
programme.desc: On July 20, 2018, double bassist Christian McBride presented his band ‘New Jawn' at the Malta Jazz Festival. A five-time Grammy winner, McBride is one of the most requested, most recorded, and most respected figures in the music world today. Hailing from Philadelphia, this music luminary combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical. Gracing the Malta Jazz stage with him are Nasheet Waits (drums), Marcus Strickland (tenor sax), and Josh Evans (trumpet).

programme.title: Al McKay & Earth Wind & Fire Experience
programme.sub-title: Al McKay's Earth, Wind & Fire Experience
programme.desc: Every year since 1970, the German city of Burghausen has been hosting one of the largest jazz festivals in the world. During Burghausen International Jazz Week, Burghausen becomes a 'Bavarian jazz mecca': guests from all over the world join the Burghausers to enjoy the most wonderful jazz performances as the colorful hustle and bustle of spectators and musicians shake up the city. One of the bands performing here in 2019 is The Earth, Wind & Fire Experience. In this concert, original Earth, Wind & Fire guitarist, writer and musical director Al McKay performs the band's timeless disco and soul. Together with seven of the thirteen original Earth, Wind & Fire members, he keep hits such as Boogie Wonderland and September alive, touring all over the world.

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie, Tony Coe, Derek Humble & Billy Mitchell
programme.desc: American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Dizzy Gillespie (1917-1993) was one of the seminal figures of the bebop movement. He fuses all musical forms rooted in African culture, such as music from Cuba, Latin America and the Caribbean, into his music. On November 4, 1970 he played a concert in Denmark with the Kenny Clarke/Francy Boland Big Band, performing Con Alma, Brother K, Now Hear My Meanin', Manteca, Let Me Outta Here, and Things Are Here.

programme.title: Larry Graham
programme.sub-title: Larry Graham: Ultimate Funk at the Bataclan
programme.desc: This recording brings a great performance by Larry Graham & Graham Central Station from the intimate Trianon in Paris. Six hundred ‘funkateers' came together to cheer the bass player and funk hero. In the sixties, Graham was mainly known for his work with Sly and the Family Stone, the popular and influential psychedelic soul and funk band. As founder and front man of Graham Central Station he has also enjoyed a successful solo career. It is often said that Graham pioneered the art of slap-pop playing on the electric bass, which has become a staple of modern funk. During this Paris performance, Graham shows he is one of the heroes of funk!

programme.title: Cannonball Adderley
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Tony Bennett
programme.desc: Relive the magic of one of the most beloved crooners in music history in his prime: Tony Bennett! Recorded live in London's Royal Albert Hall on March 2, 1974, this enchanting performance showcases Bennett's remarkable vocal prowess and charisma to full effect. Bennett is accompanied by his musical director Torrie Zito and an orchestra of British musicians that includes Kenny Clare (drums), Don Lusher (trombone), and Kenny Baker (trumpet). Tune in for Bennett's captivating take on songs “The Good Things in Life”, “My Favorite Things”, “My Love”, “I've Got Five Dollars”, “It Don't Mean a Thing”, and “Maybe This Time” in this magical concert recording.

programme.title: Ludwig Wicki, Danish National Symphony Orchestra, DR Junior Choir & Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger
programme.desc: Swiss conductor Ludwig Wicki leads the Danish National Symphony Orchestra and the DR Junior Choir in a sparkling concert program of film music. The program features a selection of music from great and Oscar-winning Hollywood films, including Titanic, The Bodyguard, Pirates of the Caribbean, Braveheart, and Moulin Rouge, written by major composers such as John Williams, Hans Zimmer, James Horner, and Klaus Badelt. Highlights include ‘He's a pirate/Davy Jones' from Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest ‘Now We Are Free' from Gladiator ‘My Heart Will Go On' from Titanic and ‘Shallow' from A Star Is Born. Featured soloists in this concert are the young talented singers Andrea Lykke Oehlenschlæger and Diluckshan Jeyaratnam. This performance was recorded at the DR Koncerthuset in Copenhagen, Denmark, in May 2022.

programme.title: Pavel Milyukov & Boris Berezovsky
programme.sub-title: Works for violin by Franck, Fauré, Schubert a.o.
programme.desc: Russian violinist Pavel Milyukov and pianist Boris Berezovsky join forces in this concert recorded at Tchaikovsky Concert Hall in Moscow, Russia, on October 15, 2020. Milyukov has won prizes at various international competitions and has appeared at venues throughout Europe. He forms an excellent chamber music duo with acclaimed pianist Boris Berezovsky. On this concert's program are César Franck's Sonata for violin and piano Gabriel Fauré's Après un rêve Alfred Schnittke's Stille Nacht and Franz Schubert's Erlkönig. As an encore, the duo performs ‘Russian Song' from Igor Stravinsky's opera Mavra (arranged by S. Dushkin).

programme.title: Daniel Barenboim
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Les Dissonances & David Grimal
programme.sub-title: Brahms - Symphony No. 2 in D major, Op. 73
programme.desc: Les Dissonances is a collective of artists founded by violinist David Grimal in 2004. The conductorless ensemble consists of musicians from the most prestigious European orchestras, international soloists, and young talents. In this performance, Les Dissonances present Johannes Brahms's Symphony No. 2 in D major, Op. 73. Whereas it took the composer over a decade to complete his first symphony, he penned his Symphony No. 2 in just a few months, during his summer sojourn in the restful Austrian village of Pörtschach in 1877. Nicknamed his ‘Pastoral' symphony, the work is arguably the most sunny and optimistic of Brahms's four symphonies. Lyrical in character, it features joyful melodies. This performance was recorded at Philharmonie de Paris, France, in 2015.

programme.title: Alexander Ullman
programme.desc: Alexander Ullman (1991, United Kingdom) performs Franz Liszt's Piano Concerto No. 2 (S125) during the final of the 11th International Franz Liszt Piano Competition, held in TivoliVredenburg, Utrecht 2017. The competition actively presents, develops and promotes piano talents from around the world. In doing so, it has become one of the prominent gateways to the international professional classical music scene for young musicians. The International Franz Liszt Piano Competition was founded in 1986 in the Netherlands and since has built a reputation as one of the world's most prestigious piano competitions.

programme.title: Evgeny Kissin, Warsaw Philharmonic Orchestra & Antoni Wit
programme.sub-title: Chopin - Piano Concerto No. 2, Op. 21 - I.
programme.desc: Antoni Wit conducts the Warsaw Philharmonic Orchestra in performances of Chopin's Piano Concertos No. 1, Op. 11 and No. 2, Op. 21. Soloists are Nikolai Demidenko and Evgeny Kissin. After performing the first concerto, Demidenko encores with Chopin's Mazurka Op. 17/4. Kissin closes the concert with Chopin's Etude Op. 10/12 and the Waltz in E minor, Op. post. This concert was part of the celebration of Chopin's 200th anniversary in 2010. Chopin composed the second concerto one year before the Concerto No. 1 and completed both works at the age of 20, before leaving his home country Poland and moving to Paris.

programme.title: Evgeny Kissin
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: David Grimal & Les Dissonances
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67
programme.desc: Les Dissonances is a collective of artists founded by violinist David Grimal in 2004. The conductorless ensemble consists of musicians from the most prestigious European orchestras, international soloists, and young talents. In this performance, Les Dissonances perform Ludwig van Beethoven's Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67. Beethoven composed Symphony No. 5 between 1804 and 1808, completing the work at nearly the same time as his Symphony No. 6, ‘Pastorale'. Both symphonies premiered as part of an all-Beethoven program at the same concert on December 22, 1808. Symphony No. 5 has become one of the best-known compositions in classical music. It opens with the famous four-note motif, often interpreted as the musical manifestation of 'fate knocking at the door'. This rhythmic figure returns in various guises in the other three movements of the symphony. The second movement consists of a set of lyrical variations on two themes. The third movement begins gently, but leads to a blasting horn section presenting a theme that features the rhythmic pattern of the four-note motif. The movement leads uninterrupted to the fourth movement that features a triumphant finale ending in C major. This performance was recorded at Philharmonie de Paris, France, in 2015.

programme.title: Dire Straits
programme.desc: During the recording of Dire Straits' third album, Making Movies, David Knopfler suddenly left the band, bringing a permanent end to the brothers' partnership within the group. As the 80s began, the band made use of the rise of music television to promote making movies and their subsequent output. Dire Straits finally dissolved in 1995, after Mark decided he wanted to focus on his solo career with no plans to re-form the band. Featuring archive material, music videos “Brothers in Arms” “Calling Elvis” and, exclusive insights from music critics and journalists Michael Bonner (Uncut Magazine), Hamish MacBain (Shortlist), John Aizlewood (Evening Standard). Rock Legends Dire Straits tells the story of one of Britain's enduring rock bands.

programme.title: Creedence Clearwater Revival
programme.desc: Thanks largely to John Fogerty's rough, inimitable voice and seemingly bottomless supply of great melodies, Creedence Clearwater Revival were the preeminent American singles band of the late Sixties and early Seventies. Leading music critics cast fresh light on the career of Creedence Clearwater Revival.

programme.title: Kiss
programme.desc: Kiss is one of the legendary groups in Hard Rock history. They have millions of fans around the world and over 100 million records sold. KISS confirms why they have been the best on the scene for nearly four decades, in a performance that made history, impressing an audience of more than 85,000 people, in a show of more than 2 hours and packed with hits, held at the Nurburgring, Germany in 2010.

programme.desc: "Flying over Fiji Islands 2" offers a 1-hour and 42-minute journey across Fiji's majestic landscapes. Combining nature sounds with ambient music by The Cynic Project, it features smooth ground-level and aerial shots, concluding with a breathtaking real-time sunset over the sea.

programme.title: Rainbow Reef Relaxation II 1
programme.desc: Immerse your senses on an unparalleled underwater journey to some of the world's most vibrant coral reefs and undersea paradises as seen in true 4K Ultra High Definition, paired to soothing new music by Relax Moods. Viewers will discover a plethora of stunning sea creatures, thanks to the incredible underwater cinematography by Peter Schneider filmed over the last several years.

programme.title: Return to Paradise 2
programme.desc: Escape to one of the world's most idyllic tropical islands for 2 hours of relaxing on the beach with "Return to Paradise,." The sequel to "A Day in Paradise" (2014), the film returns viewers to the hidden exotic Fijian island of Nanuku - a tiny palm-covered sandy atoll surrounded by magical lagoons brimming with sea life. The film lasts for 2 full hours and features a dynamic mix of stunning tropical island beach & palm tree scenes that will gently transport viewers around the island. Each scene lasts several minutes, allowing time to soak up the views & absorb the healing energies which paradise provides! There are also some aerial drone clips to give the viewer perspective from above.

programme.title: Moalboal White Beach: Philippines
programme.desc: Known to locals as Basdaku Beach, the name translates to "big sand," and it's no surprise why. Step into this expansive, white sandy beach of crystal clear waters and lose yourself in the never-ending kaleidoscope horizon of changing hues.

programme.title: Haast falls: New Zealand
programme.desc: Escape to the Haast Pass, the iconic peak home to these roaring falls of melted glacial waters. Catch a glimpse of mysteriously misty forests, gorgeous nature trails, and even local wildlife if you're lucky.

programme.title: Hawaii Sunrise Waves
programme.desc: Relax and help quiet your monkey mind with this beautiful sunrise scene filmed along the eastern shores of Oahu, Hawaii. Waves crash and glisten as the golden sun casts its warming rays, which looks best when viewed in 4K Ultra High Definition.

programme.title: Cape Reinga & Giant Sand Dunes: New Zealand
programme.desc: Take in the idyllic sights of giant sand dunes surrounded by a snaking river and greenery.

programme.title: Coastal Windmill Relaxation
programme.desc: Coastal Windmills is a 1 hour dynamic aerial relaxation film paired with calming ambient music which takes viewers to 2 different beautiful South Australia Wind Farms. Special thanks to Elliot & Des Manning for filming & editing as well as music by Christopher Lloyd Clarke of EnlightenedAudio.com. Filmed at South Australia's Cape Jervis, overlooking Saint Vincent Gulf - and Woakwine Range.

programme.title: Summer Fun I
programme.desc: Summer Fun E1: Sizzling, relaxing visual vacation.

programme.title: Happy Hour
programme.desc: Happy Hour E3: Oktoberfest — Immerse yourself in the lively spirit of Oktoberfest and the camaraderie of happy hour. With a blend of vivid colors and intricate details, this art collection transports you to bustling beer gardens, traditional attire, and clinking glasses.

programme.title: Happy Hour IV
programme.desc: Happy Hour E4 — This art collection masterfully captures life's vibrancy, alive with the exuberant energy of happy hour. Each piece dances with color and movement complementing the refreshing and illuminative mid-tempo tunes of strings, keys, synth leads, and bass.

programme.title: Happy Hour
programme.desc: Fashion Edge E1 — Step into a realm of electrifying fashion and avant-garde creativity with this high-energy collection. Unleashing the boundless imagination with cutting-edge art that redefines fashion's limits and leaves an indelible mark on the world of style.

programme.title: Featured Artists
programme.desc: Featured Artists E8: Ragni Agarwal — This vibrant collection by Indian-American artist Ragni Agarwal bursts with bold colors and pop art flair. Celebrating neurodivergence, inclusion, and unconventional beauty, her work honors the weird, the wild, and those who bloom in their own time.

programme.title: Featured Artist: Varvara Alay
programme.desc: Featured Artist: Varvara Alay — Explore the mesmerizing world of Varvara Alay, an award-winning graphic designer and illustrator. Her bold colors, surreal motifs, and intricate patterns blend fantasy and realism, creating endlessly fascinating, dreamlike realms filled with elaborate characters and majestic designs.

programme.title: Featured Artists
programme.desc: Black Artists Now E2 — Discover a captivating art collection honoring the richness and diversity of black culture, inviting audiences to embrace diversity and appreciate the extraordinary creativity of artists around the world.

programme.title: Featured Artists
programme.desc: Black Artists Now E1 — Discover a captivating art collection honoring the richness and diversity of black culture, inviting audiences to embrace diversity and appreciate the extraordinary creativity of artists around the world.

programme.title: Zen Evenings: Man Cave II, Mech Marvels
programme.desc: Zen Evenings: Man Cave E2 Mech Marvels — Where the beauty of modern engineering takes center stage and captures the essence of speed, innovation, and timeless design. Elements of jazz, funk, and alternative hip-hop create a mid to down tempo flow perfect for those looking to relax in style.
